memoryArtifacts.headline: Preserving My Version of the Guillaume II "Talvas" de Bellême Life Sketch
memoryArtifacts.description:
A couple of people are determined to destroy this line; so, I guess it's time to document this stuff and try to lock some of it into place so that they can't change it.

Guillaume II "Talvas" de Bellê …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume II Talvas於0995年出生在法國諾門迪奧內貝爾e時，他的父親Guillaume I de Bellême25歲，母親Mathilde de Ganelon26歲。他於1024年之前與Hildeburg在法國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於從1050年到1054年在法國巴-諾門迪奧內阿倫c。

nameMeanings.name: Talvas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Andre, Emile, Armand, Patrice, Raoul, Yves.

variants.name: Salva
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Aida, Marina, Alfonso, Blanca, Carlos, Fernando, Gustavo, Jaime, Jose, Juan, Juana, Julio.
Catalan (Salvà): from the personal name Salvà, Catalan form of Latin Silvanus (a derivative of silva ‘wood’).
Italian (northern): from a short form of the personal name   Salvato  .

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume II Talvas was born in 0995, in Bellême, Orne, Normandy, France, his father, Guillaume I de Bellême, was 25 and his mother, Mathilde de Ganelon, was 26. He married Hildeburg before 1024, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died from 1050 to 1054, in Alençon, Orne, Lower Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume II Talvas est né en 0995, à Bellême, Orne, Normandie, France, son père, Guillaume I de Bellême, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mathilde de Ganelon, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Hildeburg avant 1024, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé du 1050 au 1054, à Alençon, Orne, Basse-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume II Talvas 0995 in Bellême, Orne, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillaume I de Bellême 25 und seine Mutter Mathilde de Ganelon 26. Er hat vor 1024 in Frankreich Hildeburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb von 1050 bis 1054 in Alençon, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume II Talvas è nato il nel 0995 nel Bellême, Orne, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Guillaume I de Bellême, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde de Ganelon, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Hildeburg prima del 1024, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì da 1050 a 1054, nel Alençon, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume II Talvasが0995年にフランスノーマンディオルンベルeメで生まれた時，父Guillaume I de Bellêmeは25歳，母Mathilde de Ganelonは26歳であった。1024年以前にフランスでHildeburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はから1050年まで1054年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンアレンcオンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume II Talvas(이)가 0995년 프랑스 노먼디 른 브e에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillaume I de Bellême(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde de Ganelon(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1024년 이전 프랑스에서 Hildeburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1050년 부터 1054년 까지 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 알렌c에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume II Talvas nasceu em 0995, em Bellême, Orne, Normandie, França, seu pai, Guillaume I de Bellême, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde de Ganelon, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Hildeburg antes 1024, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu de 1050 a 1054, em Alençon, Orne, Basse-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume II Talvas родился в 0995 г. в местности беллeм, орн, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Guillaume I de Bellême было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilde de Ganelon, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Hildeburg перед 1024 г. в местности Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер с 1050 по 1054 в местности аленcон, орн, бассе-норманди, Франция.

sm.van 1050 to 1054: mai le 1050 e o'o I le 1054

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume II Talvas nació en 0995, en Bellême, Orne, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Guillaume I de Bellême, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mathilde de Ganelon, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Hildeburg antes de 1024, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió desde 1050 hasta 1054, en Alençon, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia.
